summary: American photorealist painter known for vanitas still-life paintings that incorporate symbolic objects like skulls, candles, and photographs. Key member of the photorealism movement alongside Chuck Close and Richard Estes. Her Vanitas series, including Marilyn (Vanitas) and Wheel of Fortune, updates the Dutch Golden Age genre with contemporary imagery.

works.name: Vanitas series (including Marilyn (Vanitas))
works.description: A series of photorealistic vanitas still-life paintings. The Marilyn entry includes photographs of Marilyn Monroe alongside traditional vanitas symbols. Inspired in part by David Bailly's Dutch Golden Age vanitas paintings. Wikipedia

works.name: Wheel of Fortune
works.description: A vanitas painting that juxtaposes an hourglass, a candle, a calendar, tarot cards, lipstick, and a skull. A modern memento mori. Wikipedia

A work in this movement by Audrey Flack, Wheel of Fortune, is a modern memento mori, including tarot cards, lipstick, and a skull.

comprehensive_summary: Audrey Flack is an American photorealist painter best known for her vanitas still-life paintings. In quiz bowl, she most commonly appears in connection with the photorealism movement, frequently grouped with Chuck Close and Richard Estes. Her signature works are her Vanitas series, which updates the Dutch Golden Age vanitas genre with contemporary imagery — most notably Marilyn (Vanitas), which incorporates photographs of Marilyn Monroe and a candlestick reflecting James Dean's image. Her Wheel of Fortune is another major vanitas work featuring symbolic objects like an hourglass, candle, tarot cards, lipstick, and a skull. Flack also appears in questions about Mary Beth Edelson's Some Living American Women Artists collage, where she is notably one of the few subjects shown in color photography. Flack's vanitas paintings were partly inspired by David Bailly's Dutch Golden Age works in the genre.
